title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs layered undo with restore, reset, revert, and references from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
Predict soft, mixed, hard, merge, keep, path-limited, and intent-to-add reset effects on HEAD, index, and worktree.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G79 is a review in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

Inspect the Gita Undo Recovery Verification trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.

content.text: For Gita Undo Recovery Verification, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Gita Undo Recovery Verification precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G79 is a review in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successful exit status proves the undo restored the intended system behavior.

Artifact under review: layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Undo Recovery Verification, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Gita Undo Recovery Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G86 is a review in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.

hints: Name the Gita Undo Recovery Verification precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
